vibe_report.summary: A refined, low-contrast monochrome palette layered with warm wood and brass accents. The overall direction is meditative and textural: matte black walls and smoked oak slatted panels anchor low-profile furniture, amber cove and recessed strip lights provide warm contrast to exterior daylight, and sparse sculptural objects (bonsai, ceramic vessels, a single bookshelf) give the scenes a calm, curated feel. Maintain architectural fidelity, keep verticals true, and derive all light from visible windows and practical fixtures.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: A base of deep charcoals and near-black (anchors and negative space) balanced by mid-tone warm wood (smoked oak/walnut) and restrained brass highlights for small accents. A pale neutral linen/stone acts only as occasional contrast to prevent loss of detail in shadowed areas.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Dramatic, directional, editorial. Primary illumination comes from exterior windows (daylight) with controlled warm supplement from amber cove/strip LEDs and a few sculptural brass lamp practicals. Shadows should be crisp and sculptural; avoid flat or evenly lit interiors.
materials: warm brass fixtures and lamp bases
textures: soft wool rugs in muted charcoal tones
furniture_language: solid slab coffee tables (monolithic black stone)
minimal open bookshelves with curated volumes
slab dining table with slender brass/wood pendant
integrated built-ins with vertical slat faces
styling_rules: Keep furnishings low and horizontal to emphasize calm planes.
Limit palette to five primary neutrals/accents; no saturated color pops beyond subtle brass or wood grain.
Curate accessories: one bonsai or sculptural object per vignette, a small stack of books, one ceramic vessel.
Use warm amber strip lighting behind slats or headboards — always visible as a practical source.
Preserve visible light-source logic: every warm accent must be explained by a visible fixture or recessed strip.
avoid: added windows or light sources not on the floor plan

room_guidance.headline: Japandi Noir Living — low, meditative, & sculptural
room_guidance.guidance: Stage a low charcoal linen sectional centered on a large muted rug facing the bay/exterior glazing. Add a monolithic black stone coffee table with a single bonsai and one stack of books. Smoked oak vertical slat panel on the main wall with recessed amber cove behind the slats. Use frosted shoji panel as a soft partition (if plan allows) to frame light without adding new openings. Include a recessed floor lamp or brass table lamp as a visible warm practical.
must_include: smoked oak vertical slat wall with amber cove

room_guidance.headline: Primary Bedroom — platform calm with strip light drama
room_guidance.guidance: Low walnut platform bed with charcoal linens and textured throws. Fluted headboard panel or vertical slats behind the bed with a recessed warm LED strip that reads as the practical light source. Brass dome bedside lamp on one side only for asymmetry. Keep bedside surfaces minimal: one ceramic tray and one book.
must_include: amber recessed LED strip behind headboard
must_avoid: excess bedside lamps or pendant clusters

room_guidance.headline: Minimal dark kitchen — tactile surfaces, warm accents
room_guidance.guidance: Matte black cabinet faces with warm wood open shelving and a narrow, integrated bookshelf niche for cookbooks. Countertops in honed dark stone. Add a single pendant in satin brass above the sink or prep area and under-cabinet warm strip light to create practical amber fills. Keep styling minimal: one ceramic pitcher, a small stack of plates, and 3–4 curated books on the shelf.

room_guidance.headline: Bay dining vignette — intimate, warm, and sculptural
room_guidance.guidance: A low-profile rectangular dining table in smoked oak with slender dark chairs. Pendant cluster: one low satin-brass pendant centered above the table (visible practical). Bay windows allow directional daylight; add amber strip recessed in the bay seat or ceiling reveal for evening mood. Keep tabletop minimal: a single fluted ceramic vessel or a small stack of books.
must_include: bay window seating or reveal with amber strip

room_guidance.headline: Guest/secondary bedroom — refined restraint
room_guidance.guidance: Smaller platform bed with charcoal linens, a narrow bookshelf against the wall (to meet 'bookshelf' requirement), and a single sculptural lamp. Vertical slat detail as a partial headboard is optional. Keep accessories sparse to preserve calm.

room_guidance.headline: Compact restful room — textured and minimal
room_guidance.guidance: Simple low bed or daybed with charcoal linen, a single wall-mounted reading lamp (brass), and a small open shelf for books. Use a narrow smoked-oak slatted accent to tie back to main palette. Keep circulation clear — minimal furniture.
